archetype: 5 (Luxury / Cinematic Dark Fantasy) mixed with 7 (Electric / Neon accents for magic)
concept: WoW-inspired Incremental RPG Web Game with TON Blockchain economy. The UI must feel epic, chunky, and tactile. Instead of a sterile SaaS dashboard, it should feel like an adventurer's command center: dark stone textures, ornate bronze/gold frames, parchment backgrounds for text, and vibrant glowing rarity colors for items.

headings.usage: Zone names, panel titles, character name, big level numbers

body.usage: Quest text, item descriptions, general UI labels

numbers.usage: DPS, TON/USDT balances, item stats, damage numbers

layout_and_spacing.strategy: 3-Column Desktop Grid for Incremental Gameplay. Center is the primary combat action. Left and right are management panels.

desktop.left_panel: col-span-3 (Character Stats, Equipment Slots, Battle Meter)
desktop.center_panel: col-span-6 (Combat Screen, Monster Sprite, Wave Counter, Action Bar)

mobile.behavior: Center combat view stays at top. Management panels become tabs/accordions below combat to save space.

layout_and_spacing.containers: Chunky borders (e.g., border-2 or border-4) with border-border_frame. Inner shadow (shadow-inner) to create a recessed look for item slots. Padding p-4 to p-6.

components.panels: Dark stone/metal colored backgrounds. Add a subtle 1px top border of lighter gray to give a 3D beveled edge effect. Never use pure transparent.
components.buttons: Action buttons should have a gradient background (e.g., bg-gradient-to-b from-amber-600 to-amber-800) with a dark border. On hover, increase brightness and add a drop-shadow glow.
components.item_slots: Square containers with bg-black/40, inset shadows. When an item is slotted, the border color must match the item's rarity.
components.progress_bars: Health (Red), XP (Purple), and Battle Meter (Gold). Must have a dark background track and a bright, animated fill.
components.wallet_module: Top right corner. Shows TON and USDT icons with monospace balances. Connect button uses a distinct glowing blue (TON brand color) to stand out from game UI.

motion_and_interaction.combat: Monster sprite should gently float/breathe (translate-y-1 up and down). On hit, the monster should quickly flash white and shake.
motion_and_interaction.damage_numbers: Floating text that spawns on hit, moves upwards, fades out, and disappears after 1s.
motion_and_interaction.hover: Hovering items in inventory should show a rich tooltip with stats, lore, and rarity colored title.
motion_and_interaction.transitions: Panel tab switching should be instant or have a very fast (150ms) fade.

background_texture.description: Dark brown/gray rock formation. Use as an overlay with low opacity (10-20%) on the body or panels to give a stone texture.

player_avatar.description: Knight in full armor, use for the character portrait in the left panel.

monster_orc.description: Orc monster figure holding an axe. Use as the primary enemy sprite in the center combat view.

loot_weapon.description: Glowing green abstract shape. Use as a placeholder for a magical rune weapon in the inventory/shop.

testing_rules.data_testid: ALL interactive elements (attack buttons, inventory slots, wallet connect, tab switches, upgrade buttons) MUST have a 'data-testid' attribute using kebab-case role-based naming (e.g., 'combat-attack-button', 'inventory-slot-1', 'wallet-connect-button').

instructions_to_main_agent: Install and use 'lucide-react' for UI icons (swords, shields, coins, settings, etc).
Implement the TonConnect SDK for the wallet module.
Set up a centralized store or context for the game state (Gold, TON, USDT, Player Stats, Wave Level).
Build the bilingual system (EN/PT) using an i18n library or a simple context dictionary.

Do NOT use basic Shadcn cards for the main layout; build custom HTML/Tailwind panels with thick borders and textures to achieve the WoW/RPG aesthetic. You can use Shadcn for complex logic (Dropdowns, Dialogs for shop).
Implement the 'Battle Meter' (BM) as an aggregate score of all equipped item stats and talent nodes.
Ensure the combat loop works automatically or via manual clicking (incremental style).
